paper.title: Yin Yang 1 activates JAK-STAT3-mediated epithelial-mesenchymal transition in Helicobacter pylori-induced gastric cancer progression

entities.name: STAT3
synonyms: signal transducer and activator of transcription 3

entities.name: Yin Yang 1 activates JAK-STAT3-mediated epithelial-mesenchymal transition in Helicobacter pylori-induced gastric cancer progression

evidence_entities.text: YY1 was highly expressed in gastric cancer tissues and cells. Kaplan-Meier survival curves indicated that high YY1 expression correlated with a poor prognosis.
In vivo and in vitro experiments demonstrated that H. pylori infection promoted phosphorylation of JAK2 and STAT3, thereby activating the EMT pathway, in which YY1 played a key role.
YY1 and JAK2 interaction was validated by chromatin immunoprecipitation.
YY1 knockdown or pharmacological inhibition reversed EMT and suppressed gastric cancer cell proliferation and metastasis.
Western blotting demonstrated time-dependent upregulation of YY1, along with the EMT mesenchymal markers N-cadherin, Snail, and vimentin, in both cell lines.
Colony formation and 5-ethynyl-2'-deoxyuridine incorporation assays demonstrated that YY1 overexpression markedly enhanced cell proliferation, whereas YY1 knockdown significantly suppressed it.
Transwell and wound-healing assays further revealed that YY1 promoted cell migration and invasion, while its depletion attenuated these malignant phenotypes.
JASPAR further predicted putative YY1 binding sites within the JAK2 promoter region, which was validated by ChIP assays confirming direct YY1 binding.
